LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE , SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO NEWLY FILED AY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. THE COURT'S TENTATIVE DECISION FROM THE FEBRUARY 2ND HEARING IS THE DECISION OF THIS COURT. AS TO THE GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. ALTHOUGH THE NAVY MAY HAVE BEEN INVOLVED IN TESTING THE UNIBESTOS, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE GOVERNMENT DESIGNED THE UNIBESTOS, OR THAT THE UNIBESTOS WAS ANYTHING OTHER THAN THE PRODUCT READILY AVAILABLE TO COMMERCIAL USERS. SEE, IN RE HAWAII FEDERAL ASBESTOS CASES 960 F2D 806 (9TH CIR. 1992). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-18-2008 IN 206, PLTF'S MOTION FOR SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL WITH PREFERENCE STATUS AND SEVERING CASE FROM GROUP IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A)&(D). THIS CASE IS SEVERED FROM BRAYTON GRP 469 AND SET SOLO FOR TRIAL. TRIAL DATE OF 2/17/09 IS MAINTAINED. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET ON 1/20/09 IN DEPT. 611 IS VACATED. SETTLEMENT CONF. IS SET IN DEPT 220. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLTF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFT BY 12/19/08. IF PLTF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, TO PLTF BY 1/12/09. IF DEFTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LESS THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LESS THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO 3 EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE , SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Lisa L. Oberg, of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP, (415) 267-4000, appearing for Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation and moving party. There are no other appearances. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's objection to newly filed Ay Declaration is sustained. The Court's tentative decision from the February 2nd hearing is the decision of this Court. As to the government contractor defense, defendant fails to shift the burden. Although the Navy may have been involved in testing the Unibestos, there is no showing that the government designed the Unibestos, or that the Unibestos was anything other than the product readily available to commercial users. See, In re Hawaii Federal Asbestos Cases 960 F2d 806 (9th Cir. 1992). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Sara M. Parker, Esq., and Lisa Oberg, Esq., of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP, (415) 267-4117, appearing for Defendant Metalclad, Insulation Company Corporation and moving party; Ron Archer, Esq., of Brayton Purcell, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. The Court hears argument regarding the government contractor defense issue. Defendant Metalclad's motion for summary judgment as to the government contractor defense issue is continued to February 9, 2009.
The Court rules as follows as to the Evidentiary Objections: Defendant's Objections 1, 2, 3, and 11 thru 16, are sustained; Defendant's objections, 4-10; and 17 & 18 are overruled. Plaintiff's objections to the Depositions of Wilfred Newton and Dan Helfin are sustained. (There was no proposed Order provided to the Court regarding evidentiary objections, pursuant to CRC 3.1352.)
As to the Motion for Summary Adjudication, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and strict liability. Summary adjudication is Granted as to false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
